excluded-macos: Darwin has no PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER equivalent, so an orphaned grandchild reparents to launchd and the engine cannot harvest it. Linux engine forwards to the host and still runs+passes this.
CLONE_CHILD_SETTID/CLEARTID writes the tid and wakes the CLEARTID futex on exit (engine hangs waiting on the clear-tid futex)
CLONE_VM|CLONE_VFORK shares the address space so child writes are visible to the parent (engine breaks the shared-VM/vfork contract)
excluded-macos: prctl pdeathsig/name, SIGCHLD siginfo, pidfd-getfd and reparent topology diverge on the macOS engine process model; Linux engine still runs+enforces this.
Sparse close_range through UINT_MAX completes promptly in a background child, as required by Erlang port startup
execve errno classes: directory target gives EACCES/EISDIR and a non-ELF file gives ENOEXEC (fixed cc23acb3a; a later merge stale-reverted this row to excluded and dropped the gate)

root/unprivileged credential+cap invariants: getuid/getresuid/status agree, setter privilege model (drop reflected/irreversible when root, EPERM raise otherwise), capset/capbset/nnp/keepcaps/securebits round-trip (PR_GET_SECUREBITS returned EINVAL before the fix)

sched_setattr/sched_getattr consistency: getattr sched_policy agrees with sched_getscheduler, setattr records the policy and rejects RT with EPERM like sched_setscheduler, getattr sched_nice tracks the live process nice (getattr hardcoded SCHED_OTHER+nice 0 and setattr was a blanket no-op before the fix)

job-control stop/continue wait-status: WUNTRACED WIFSTOPPED/WSTOPSIG + WCONTINUED WIFCONTINUED(0xffff), and a SIGSTOP/SIGTSTP-stopped child that is SIGCONT-continued must RESUME and report its real WEXITSTATUS (engine forced WIFEXITED 128+stopsig e.g. 0x9300 on continue, losing the exit code); waitid CLD_STOPPED/CONTINUED/EXITED/KILLED siginfo + WNOWAIT re-reap + waitpid pid selection/ECHILD

PR_MCE_KILL/PR_MCE_KILL_GET policy round-trip, PR_TASK_PERF_EVENTS_DISABLE/ENABLE always succeed, and PR_SET_MM is EPERM without CAP_SYS_RESOURCE (was silently returning 0)

PR_CAP_AMBIENT arg/policy surface: RAISE is EPERM because the inheritable set is empty (engine used to return 0, falsely granting the ambient cap); LOWER/IS_SET/CLEAR_ALL and the EINVAL edges round-trip
POKEDATA writeback observed through the tracee's own write(); SETREGSET(NT_PRSTATUS) round-trip; FP register-set request and a bogus request both fail cleanly (no crash, no fake success)
PTRACE_ATTACH/GETREGSET/DETACH against a running child, ATTACH to a nonexistent pid returns ESRCH, and PTRACE_CONT injects SIGUSR1 (handler exit 88)

PTRACE_ATTACH to a tracee blocked inside a host syscall (pause) does not interrupt it, so the group-stop is never delivered; needs async cross-process signal-delivery (signal.c) wired into the attach path. Engine does not crash/wedge; self-bounded by alarm(4). Native golden is the correct behavior.

thread-group teardown and exit-status encoding: exit_group over blocked siblings, per-thread SYS_exit survival, pthread_exit last-thread, thread SIGSEGV group kill, exit()==exit_group, abort SIGABRT, exit(261) low-8-bits
excluded-macos: prctl pdeathsig/name, SIGCHLD siginfo, pidfd-getfd and reparent topology diverge on the macOS engine process model; Linux engine still runs+enforces this.

double-fork daemonize / subshell-pipeline shape: a glibc fork() from an already glibc-forked child. Was x86-only corrupt (fork child preserved the parent's warm translations, so a nested fork's parent continuation resolved through a sibling generation's inherited direct block chain -> corrupted libc return / stack-guard trip). Fixed by dropping inherited translations in the Linux fork child (jit_after_fork preserve=0), matching aarch64
an orphaned process group with a stopped member gets SIGHUP+SIGCONT when it becomes orphaned (the controlling parent exits) so stopped jobs cannot hang forever

single-threaded-parent fork fast path: child spawns threads driving a private futex + eventfd + nested fork, proving the skipped thread_after_fork reset leaves the inherited private-futex table correct
descriptor inheritance across execve of /proc/self/exe with O_CLOEXEC, F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC, dup2 and F_SETFD; native Linux oracle
capget/capset version negotiation: unsupported version EINVAL with header rewritten to preferred v3
PR_CAPBSET_READ out-of-range EINVAL, in-range never errors, PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS monotone latch
fixed-ABI 100Hz _SC_CLK_TCK and times() success/field-sign classes, no wall-clock value asserted
getcpu/sysinfo populate well-formed out-params; only success+positivity booleans asserted, never host counts
pgid/sid error surfaces plus group-leader setsid EPERM
credential-query shapes, no absolute uid/gid values asserted
